name: Can I handle AEO strategy without a developer, or is technical help mandatory?

mainEntity.name: Can I handle AEO strategy without a developer, or is technical help mandatory?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You don’t need a developer for every AEO task, but you typically need technical help at least once to set up clean schema templates and ensure your site is easy for answer engines to crawl. After that, content and marketing teams can usually manage most ongoing AEO work.

Trying to do everything without technical support often leads to broken or inconsistent markup.

Key technical needs:
- Implementing JSON‑LD schema (FAQPage, HowTo, Article, Organization, LocalBusiness)
- Fixing crawlability, page speed, and basic site structure
- Setting up reusable schema snippets or templates in your CMS
- Monitoring for schema errors in search tools

Practically, treat technical setup as a short project and then train your content team to reuse and adapt the templates.

This collaboration keeps your AEO strategy sustainable while avoiding the risk of incorrect markup undercutting good content.
acceptedAnswer.description: You can drive much of AEO through content, but you usually need a developer at least once to set up clean JSON‑LD schema, fix crawlability issues, and create templates. After that, your marketing team can reuse those templates. Skipping technical help often leads to broken markup that undermines good answers.
